judge_frozen.rationale: Paper A reports zero-shot recognition (compared to PointCLIP/CLIP2Point), Paper B reports fine-tuned transfer learning accuracy with voting.
ScanObjectNN has multiple variants (OBJ-BG, OBJ-ONLY, PB-T50-RS); different subtasks likely explain the 4.72-point gap.
Paper A uses ResNet backbone on val set; Paper B uses ViT-B/16 with CLIP pretraining on test set—different backbone, split, and method.
Both papers report their own method as 'Ours' but they are different methods (PointNet++ vs DGCNN), making the identity match spurious.
Paper A reports test split (73.8), Paper B reports val split (76.6) for Cityscapes, explaining the 2.8-point gap.
Paper B reports few-shot (5-way 10-shot) accuracy; Paper A reports standard classification with voting. Different tasks entirely.
Paper A uses LLaVA-Next with Vicuna-7B; Paper B uses LLaVA-NeXT with 8B (LLaMA-3-8B), different model variants.
Paper B reports few-shot classification (5-way/10-way), while Paper A reports standard full fine-tuning accuracy on ModelNet40.
ScanObjectNN has multiple variants (OBJ-BG, OBJ-ONLY, PB_T50_RS); papers report different subtask variants of the same dataset.
Paper A's 70.4 is from PASCAL VOC 2012 table, not Cityscapes. Wrong dataset extracted for this canonical cell.
Paper A's 70.4 is from PASCAL VOC 2012 table (per caption), not Cityscapes. Wrong dataset extracted.
Paper A's 0.3797 is the Log loss column value, not AUC; actual AUC in Paper A's table is 0.7804, consistent with Paper B's 77.99%.
Paper A's 83.7 is from S3DIS segmentation memory table; Paper B's 80.4 is from few-shot ModelNet40 table. Neither matches ShapeNetPart mIoU.
Paper A reports +4.5 as a relative improvement delta, not an absolute mIoU score; Paper B reports 12.3 as absolute mIoU. Not comparable values.
Paper A's 29.4 is mAP@0.5 on THUMOS14 action detection, not AR@100 on ActivityNet-1.3; wrong cell extracted.
Paper A's 0.3820 is FM Avazu Log loss, not AUC; the actual AUC in that table is 0.7765, close to B's 0.7785.
Paper A explicitly reports test-dev results (44.7%), Paper B reports val set results (41.0%), different evaluation splits.
Paper B's 97.2% is from a ModelNet40 Linear SVM table, not ScanObjectNN accuracy; wrong column extracted.
Paper B's 78.8 is VQAv2, not GQA; its actual GQA value is 59.3*, matching Paper A exactly.
Both papers show identical rows; 18.4 and 30.6 are different columns (unsupervised mIoU vs linear mIoU) from the same row.
Paper A reports linear probe CLIP accuracy; Paper B reports zero-shot CLIP accuracy, different evaluation settings.
Paper A uses CLIP in a KD/compressed model context (37%), while Paper B uses standard CLIP ViT-B/16 for prompt learning (66.73%), different model/setting.
Paper A uses varying training proportions (20-100%) for IMDB; Paper B uses standard fixed benchmark split, causing incomparable results.
Paper A reports GIN with edge attributes (0.209) while Paper B reports GIN without edge attributes (0.526), different input settings.
Paper A reports PSPNet on Cityscapes test set (segmentation); Paper B reports PSPNet on a classification dataset (val), completely different tasks.
Paper B's Transformer† uses ReCon-block and fine-tuning techniques with specific point counts; Paper A uses standard ShapeNet pretraining fine-tuning.
